mnemonics: Weak Entities Wait For Foreign Keys (WEWFFK)
keyPoints: Strong entities map directly to their own table with their own primary key.
Weak entities become a table that includes the primary key of its owner as a foreign key.
Many-to-many relationships become a separate relation (table) with foreign keys from both participating entities.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to include the primary key of the owner entity in a weak entity's table.
Creating a separate table for a one-to-many relationship instead of just adding a foreign key to the 'many' side.
Not including all the attributes of a composite attribute when mapping it to a table.
